thresholds.status: Apple, Google, Meta designated; interoperability mandates active
DOJ Google search remedy proceedings underway
EU DMA portability provisions; U.S. lacks federal equivalent
GPT-4/Gemini integration into search creating new switching barriers

thresholds.domain: Global ad market >70% Big Tech share
thresholds.status: Google+Meta = ~68% of global digital ad spend in 2024

items.name: Network effects lock-in
items.description: Platform value grows superlinearly with user base, creating winner-take-most dynamics that prevent competitive entry even when incumbents degrade quality or raise prices.

items.name: Data asymmetry moat
items.description: Incumbents' training data advantage for AI and behavioral targeting is self-reinforcing: more users generate more data, improving services, attracting more users.

items.name: Regulatory capture through complexity
items.description: Platform governance requires technical expertise that regulators lack; the revolving door between Big Tech and regulatory agencies ensures that substantive enforcement is systematically understaffed.

methods.welfare_function: W measured as consumer surplus loss from monopoly pricing, privacy welfare loss (WTP studies), foregone innovation value from killer acquisitions, and attention-economy mental health costs (VSL-weighted).
methods.cooperative_baseline: Competitive platform market with mandatory interoperability, data portability, and no preferential self-dealing, generating ~$35B in legitimate platform coordination value.
key_sources: EU Digital Markets Act enforcement reports (2024)
Stigler Committee on Digital Platforms, Report (2019)
Statista, Global digital advertising market share (2024)
FTC, Study on Social Media and Video Streaming (2024)
falsification: F1: Demonstrate that platform concentration has reduced search quality or advertising costs for consumers relative to pre-concentration baselines.
F2: Show that killer acquisitions (Instagram, WhatsApp, YouTube) increased rather than suppressed downstream innovation in adjacent markets.
F3: Demonstrate that network effects cannot be overcome by regulatory interoperability mandates, citing a failed natural experiment.
